fullArticle:
The identity of the man has been suppressed. (ABC News)

A man charged over the alleged sexual assault of two students at an Adelaide Hills school has appeared in court.

The man aged in his 30s, whose identity is suppressed, is charged with two counts of indecent assault.

The man will next appear in the Adelaide Magistrates Court in November.

A contract worker who allegedly sexually assaulted two students at an Adelaide Hills school he worked at has appeared in court for the first time.

The man aged in his 30s, who cannot be named due a suppression order on his identity, is charged with two counts of indecent assault between April 2025 and May 2026.

Court documents viewed by the ABC allege two female students were indecently assaulted by the man on separate occasions.
